evidence.snippet: It provides tactical grade performance for unmanned vehicles, mobile mapping and other commercial and military guidance applications. The CPT7700 is a small, lightweight and low-power solution with multiple communication interfaces for easy integration on various platforms. The deep coupling of GNSS and INS measurements enhances satellite tracking and enables the most continuous positioning possible.

NovAtel’s Synchronous Position, Attitude and Navigation (SPAN) technology can be integrated with this IMU to provide accurate 3D position, velocity and attitude data in a compact package. This combination makes the IMU ideal to use in airborne, marine and ground applications.

SPAN® Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs)
---------------------------------------
SPAN technology deeply couples NovAtel's GNSS receiver technology with an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU). We market a variety of IMUs from leading IMU manufacturers to provide our customers the opportunity to select an IMU that delivers the accuracy or price point their application requires.

items.notes: Core business: GNSS receivers, GNSS+INS combined systems, IMUs, and GNSS antennas marketed explicitly for unmanned vehicles and precision navigation.

evidence.snippet:

Protection for every platform. GNSS Anti-Jam Technology (GAJT) antenna systems are battle-proven assured positioning, navigation and timing (APNT) solutions.
[Learn More](/products/anti-jam-antenna-systems-gajt "Learn More")

items.notes: GAJT anti-jam antenna product line and GRIT GNSS Resilience & Integrity Technology (spoofing detection, interference toolkit) are explicitly EW/counter-jamming products.

tagline: GNSS/inertial navigation and positioning components for unmanned systems: receivers, IMUs, antennas (including anti-jam CRPA variants), and post-processing software. Notable products include PwrPak7 series inertial navigation systems and GRIT spoofing/interference detection technology. NDAA compliance status and Blue UAS certification unclear from public sources. Canadian-headquartered subsidiary of Hexagon.
